---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---
# Libraries
```{r, warning=FALSE}
library(targets)
library(tidyverse)
library(visNetwork)
```


```{r}
#setwd(dirname(rstudioapi::getActiveDocumentContext()$path))
setwd(this.path::this.dir())
getwd()
```

# Check pipeline status
```{r}
tar_glimpse()
```


```{r}
tar_visnetwork()
```

```{r}
tar_visnetwork(targets_only = TRUE)
```

```{r}
tar_manifest()
tar_manifest(fields = name)$name
```

# Construct pipeline
```{r}
# targets::tar_make(callr_function = NULL) # for debugging
targets::tar_make()
```

# Destroy pipeline (off by default)
```{r, warning=FALSE, eval = FALSE }
tar_destroy()
# targets::tar_destroy(ask = FALSE)
```


# DEBUG
```{r}
tar_load(df_simNameByCult)
tar_load(df_soil_profile_clean)
tar_load(df_obs_meta_data)
tar_load(list_observed_dfs_raw)
tar_load(list_observed_dfs_clean)
tar_load(final_apsim_observed)
tar_load(df_final_observed_harv)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(targets)

# 1. Load the compiled data from the targets cache
my_raw_data <- tar_read(list_observed_dfs_raw)

# 2. Run the Date QC Test
date_qc_table <- my_raw_data %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    HasDate = purrr::map_lgl(data, function(df) {
      # Fallback 1: If the dataframe is completely empty (skipped sheet) -> FALSE
      if (is.null(df) || nrow(df) == 0) return(FALSE)
      
      # Fallback 2: If the 'Date' column doesn't exist -> FALSE
      if (!"Date" %in% names(df)) return(FALSE)
      
      # Final Check: Are there actually valid dates inside, or just NAs?
      # Returns TRUE if at least one real date exists.
      any(!is.na(df$Date))
    })
  ) %>%
  # Select only the two columns you want to see
  dplyr::select(df_name, HasDate)

# Print the final report!
date_qc_table %>% 
  as_tibble() %>% 
  print(n = Inf)
```
